compact_context.description: When true: bootstrap_max_chars=6000, rag_chunk_limit=2. Use for 13B or smaller models.

context_aware_tools.description: Enable context-aware tool filtering (only surface relevant tools per iteration).

context_compression.description: Context compression configuration for automatic conversation compaction.

history_pruning.description: History pruning configuration for token efficiency.

keep_tool_context_turns.description:
Number of most recent conversation turns whose full tool-call/result
messages are preserved in channel conversation history. Older turns
keep only the final assistant text. Set to `0` to disable (previous
behavior). Default: `2`.

max_context_tokens.description:
Maximum estimated tokens for conversation history before compaction triggers.
Uses ~4 chars/token heuristic. When this threshold is exceeded, older messages
are summarized to preserve context while staying within budget. Default: `32000`.

max_history_messages.description: Maximum conversation history messages retained per session. Default: `50`.

max_system_prompt_chars.description:
Maximum characters for the assembled system prompt. When `> 0`, the prompt
is truncated to this limit after assembly (keeping the top portion which
contains identity and safety instructions). `0` means unlimited.
Useful for small-context models (e.g. glm-4.5-air ~8K tokens → set to 8000).

max_tool_iterations.description:
Maximum tool-call loop turns per user message. Default: `10`.
Setting to `0` falls back to the safe default of `10`.

max_tool_result_chars.description:
Maximum characters for a single tool result before truncation.
Head (2/3) and tail (1/3) are preserved with a truncation marker in the
middle. Set to `0` to disable truncation. Default: `50000`.

parallel_tools.description: Enable parallel tool execution within a single iteration. Default: `false`.

thinking.description:
Thinking/reasoning level control. Configures how deeply the model reasons
per message. Users can override per-message with `/think:<level>` directives.

tool_call_dedup_exempt.description: Tools exempt from the within-turn duplicate-call dedup check. Default: `[]`.

tool_filter_groups.description:
Per-turn MCP tool schema filtering groups.

When non-empty, only MCP tools matched by an active group are included in the
tool schema sent to the LLM for that turn. Built-in tools always pass through.
Default: `[]` (no filtering — all tools included).

enabled.description: Enable audit logging

log_path.description: Path to audit log file (relative to zeroclaw dir)

max_size_mb.description: Maximum log size in MB before rotation

sign_events.description: Sign events with HMAC for tamper evidence

AutonomyConfig.description:
Autonomy and security policy configuration (`[autonomy]` section).

Controls what the agent is allowed to do: shell commands, filesystem access,
risk approval gates, and per-policy budgets.

allowed_commands.description: Allowlist of executable names permitted for shell execution.

allowed_roots.description:
Extra directory roots the agent may read/write outside the workspace.
Supports absolute, `~/...`, and workspace-relative entries.
Resolved paths under any of these roots pass `is_resolved_path_allowed`.

always_ask.description: Tools that always require interactive approval, even after "Always".

auto_approve.description: Tools that never require approval (e.g. read-only tools).

block_high_risk_commands.description: Block high-risk shell commands even if allowlisted.

forbidden_paths.description: Explicit path denylist. Default includes system-critical paths and sensitive dotdirs.

max_actions_per_hour.description: Maximum actions allowed per hour per policy. Default: `100`.

max_cost_per_day_cents.description: Maximum cost per day in cents per policy. Default: `1000`.

non_cli_excluded_tools.description:
Tools to exclude from non-CLI channels (e.g. Telegram, Discord).

When a tool is listed here, non-CLI channels will not expose it to the
model in tool specs.

require_approval_for_medium_risk.description: Require explicit approval for medium-risk shell commands.

shell_env_passthrough.description:
Additional environment variables allowed for shell tool subprocesses.

These names are explicitly allowlisted and merged with the built-in safe
baseline (`PATH`, `HOME`, etc.) after `env_clear()`.

shell_timeout_secs.description: Timeout in seconds for shell tool subprocesses. Default: 60.

workspace_only.description:
Restrict absolute filesystem paths to workspace-relative references. Default: `true`.
Resolved paths outside the workspace still require `allowed_roots`.

AutonomyLevel.description: How much autonomy the agent has

oneOf.description: Read-only: can observe but not act
Supervised: acts but requires approval for risky operations
Full: autonomous execution within policy bounds

destination_dir.description: Output directory for backup archives (relative to workspace root).

enabled.description: Enable the `backup` tool.

encrypt.description: Encrypt backup archives (requires a configured secret store key).

include_dirs.description: Workspace subdirectories to include in backups.

max_keep.description: Maximum number of backups to keep (oldest are pruned).

schedule_cron.description: Optional cron expression for scheduled automatic backups.

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

BrowserComputerUseConfig.description:
Computer-use sidecar configuration (`[browser.computer_use]` section).

Delegates OS-level mouse, keyboard, and screenshot actions to a local sidecar.

allow_remote_endpoint.description: Allow remote/public endpoint for computer-use sidecar (default: false)

api_key.description: Optional bearer token for computer-use sidecar

endpoint.description: Sidecar endpoint for computer-use actions (OS-level mouse/keyboard/screenshot)

max_coordinate_x.description: Optional X-axis boundary for coordinate-based actions

max_coordinate_y.description: Optional Y-axis boundary for coordinate-based actions

window_allowlist.description: Optional window title/process allowlist forwarded to sidecar policy

BrowserConfig.description:
Browser automation configuration (`[browser]` section).

Controls the `browser_open` tool and browser automation backends.

allowed_domains.description: Allowed domains for `browser_open` (exact or subdomain match)

enabled.description: Enable `browser_open` tool (opens URLs in the system browser without scraping)

native_chrome_path.description: Optional Chrome/Chromium executable path for rust-native backend

native_webdriver_url.description: WebDriver endpoint URL for rust-native backend (e.g. `http://127.0.0.1:9515`)

command_logger.description: Enable the command-logger hook (logs tool calls for auditing).

webhook_audit.description:
Configuration for the webhook-audit hook.

When enabled, POSTs a JSON payload to `url` for every tool invocation
that matches one of `tool_patterns`.

ack_reactions.description:
Whether to add acknowledgement reactions (👀 on receipt, ✅/⚠️ on
completion) to incoming channel messages. Default: `true`.

cli.description: Enable the CLI interactive channel. Default: `true`.

debounce_ms.description:
Inbound message debounce window in milliseconds. When a sender fires
multiple messages within this window, they are accumulated and dispatched
as a single concatenated message. `0` disables debouncing. Default: `0`.

discord_history.description: Discord history channel — logs ALL messages and forwards @mentions to agent.

gmail_push.description: Gmail Pub/Sub push notification channel configuration.

message_timeout_secs.description:
Base timeout in seconds for processing a single channel message (LLM + tools).
Runtime uses this as a per-turn budget that scales with tool-loop depth
(up to 4x, capped) so one slow/retried model call does not consume the
entire conversation budget.
Default: 300s for on-device LLMs (Ollama) which are slower than cloud APIs.

session_backend.description:
Session persistence backend: `"jsonl"` (legacy) or `"sqlite"` (new default).
SQLite provides FTS5 search, metadata tracking, and TTL cleanup.

session_persistence.description:
Persist channel conversation history to JSONL files so sessions survive
daemon restarts. Files are stored in `{workspace}/sessions/`. Default: `true`.

session_ttl_hours.description: Auto-archive stale sessions older than this many hours. `0` disables. Default: `0`.

show_tool_calls.description:
Whether to send tool-call notification messages (e.g. `🔧 web_search_tool: …`)
to channel users. When `false`, tool calls are still logged server-side but
not forwarded as individual channel messages. Default: `false`.

voice_duplex.description: Voice duplex configuration (full-duplex voice over WebSocket).

wati.description: WATI WhatsApp Business API channel configuration.

wecom.description: WeCom (WeChat Enterprise) Bot Webhook channel configuration.

whatsapp.description: WhatsApp channel configuration (Cloud API or Web mode).

ClassificationRule.description: A single classification rule mapping message patterns to a model hint.

max_length.description: Only match if message length <= N chars.

min_length.description: Only match if message length >= N chars.

priority.description: Higher priority rules are checked first.

Controls the read-only cloud transformation analysis tools:

default_cloud.description: Default cloud provider for analysis context. Default: "aws".

iac_tools.description: Supported IaC tools for review. Default: \[`terraform`\].

well_architected_frameworks.description: Well-Architected Frameworks to check against. Default: \[`aws-waf`\].

token.description: Cloudflare Tunnel token (from Zero Trust dashboard)

CodexCliConfig.description:
Codex CLI tool configuration (`[codex_cli]` section).

Delegates coding tasks to the `codex -q` CLI. Authentication uses the
binary's own session by default — no API key needed unless
`env_passthrough` includes `OPENAI_API_KEY`.

env_passthrough.description: Extra env vars passed to the codex subprocess (e.g. OPENAI_API_KEY)

max_output_bytes.description: Maximum output size in bytes (2MB default)

Composio managed OAuth tools integration (`[composio]` section).

Provides access to 1000+ OAuth-connected tools via the Composio platform.

api_key.description: Composio API key (stored encrypted when secrets.encrypt = true)

entity_id.description: Default entity ID for multi-user setups

Config.description:
Top-level ZeroClaw configuration, loaded from `config.toml`.

Resolution order: `ZEROCLAW_WORKSPACE` env → `active_workspace.toml` marker → `~/.zeroclaw/config.toml`.

agents.description: Delegate agent configurations for multi-agent workflows.

browser_delegate.description:
Browser delegation configuration (`[browser_delegate]`).

Delegates browser-based tasks to a browser-capable CLI subprocess (e.g.
Claude Code with `claude-in-chrome` MCP tools). Useful for interacting
with corporate web apps (Teams, Outlook, Jira, Confluence) that lack
direct API access. A persistent Chrome profile can be configured so SSO
sessions survive across invocations.

Fields:
- `enabled` (`bool`, default `false`) — enable the browser delegation tool.
- `cli_binary` (`String`, default `"claude"`) — CLI binary to spawn for browser tasks.
- `chrome_profile_dir` (`String`, default `""`) — Chrome user-data directory for
  persistent SSO sessions. When empty, a fresh profile is used each invocation.
- `allowed_domains` (`Vec<String>`, default `[]`) — allowlist of domains the browser
  may navigate to. Empty means all non-blocked domains are permitted.
- `blocked_domains` (`Vec<String>`, default `[]`) — denylist of domains. Blocked
  domains take precedence over allowed domains.
- `task_timeout_secs` (`u64`, default `120`) — per-task timeout in seconds.

Compatibility: additive and disabled by default; existing configs remain valid when omitted.
Rollback/migration: remove `[browser_delegate]` or keep `enabled = false` to disable.

channels.description: Channel configurations: Telegram, Discord, Slack, etc. (`[channels]`).

claude_code_runner.description: Claude Code task runner with Slack progress and SSH session handoff (`[claude_code_runner]`).

conversational_ai.description:
Conversational AI agent builder configuration (`[conversational_ai]`).

Experimental / future feature — not yet wired into the agent runtime.
Omitted from generated config files when disabled (the default).
Existing configs that already contain this section will continue to
deserialize correctly thanks to `#[serde(default)]`.

cost.description: Cost tracking and budget enforcement configuration (`[cost]`).

gateway.description: Gateway server configuration: host, port, pairing, rate limits (`[gateway]`).

heartbeat.description: Heartbeat configuration for periodic health pings (`[heartbeat]`).

hooks.description: Hooks configuration (lifecycle hooks and built-in hook toggles).

identity.description: Identity format configuration: OpenClaw or AIEOS (`[identity]`).

locale.description:
Locale for tool descriptions (e.g. `"en"`, `"zh-CN"`).

When set, tool descriptions shown in system prompts are loaded from
Fluent `.ftl` locale files. Falls back to embedded English, then to
hardcoded descriptions.

If omitted or empty, the locale is auto-detected from `ZEROCLAW_LOCALE`,
`LANG`, or `LC_ALL` environment variables (defaulting to `"en"`).

memory.description: Memory backend configuration: sqlite, markdown, embeddings (`[memory]`).

onboard_state.description:
Meta-state for `zeroclaw onboard` (which sections the user has
already walked through). Not user-facing config (`[onboard_state]`).

pacing.description: Pacing controls for slow/local LLM workloads (`[pacing]`).

peripherals.description: Peripheral board configuration for hardware integration (`[peripherals]`).

proxy.description: Proxy configuration for outbound HTTP/HTTPS/SOCKS5 traffic (`[proxy]`).

query_classification.description: Automatic query classification — maps user messages to model hints.

reliability.description: Reliability settings: retries, fallback providers, backoff (`[reliability]`).

runtime.description: Runtime adapter configuration (`[runtime]`). Controls native vs Docker execution.

scheduler.description: Scheduler configuration for periodic task execution (`[scheduler]`).

skills.description: Skills loading and community repository behavior (`[skills]`).

transcription.description: Voice transcription configuration (Whisper API via Groq).

trust.description: Trust scoring and regression detection configuration (`[trust]`).

tunnel.description: Tunnel configuration for exposing the gateway publicly (`[tunnel]`).

verifiable_intent.description: Verifiable Intent (VI) credential verification and issuance (`[verifiable_intent]`).

Config.title: Config

ConfigApiCode.description:
Stable error code consumed by HTTP / CLI / dashboard. Add codes here as new
failure cases land — never invent codes ad-hoc at call sites.

oneOf.description: The supplied property path is not defined in the schema.

Generic schema validation failure (catch-all wrapping `Config::validate()` bails).
Specific failure modes (`provider_fallback_dangling`, etc.) get their own
codes as the validator is incrementally restructured.
`providers.fallback` references a key not present in `providers.models`.

On-disk config differs from in-memory state (an out-of-band file edit
happened despite the daemon-running rule). Caller should reload.

The daemon-reload step after a successful save failed; on-disk config
has been reverted to the pre-write snapshot to keep state consistent.
JSON Patch operation type is not supported in this PR (`move` / `copy`).

JSON Patch `test` operation targeted a secret or derived-from-secret
path; rejected to prevent differential value inference.

The supplied JSON value does not match the field's declared type
(e.g. an array passed where a scalar was expected, or a non-string
element in a `Vec<String>`).

A required scalar field was empty / missing / blank.
Path identifies which one (e.g. `gateway.host`,
`tunnel.openvpn.config_file`).

A numeric field was out of its allowed range (zero, negative, or
above an upper bound). Path identifies which one.

A string did not match its allowed format — invalid URL, bad
scheme, invalid path prefix, characters outside the allowed set.

An enum / discriminator field carried a value not in the allowed
set (e.g. `tunnel.provider` with an unknown name).

A reference to another config entry pointed at something that
doesn't exist (e.g. `agents.<x>.delegate_to` naming a missing
agent). Distinct from `ProviderFallbackDangling` — that's the
specific case the dashboard surfaces for the providers section.

Catch-all server failure not classified above. Avoid in code; log the
original error and convert to a more specific code where possible.

ConfigApiError.description:
Structured error returned by the new HTTP CRUD endpoints and the `zeroclaw config`
subcommands they share infrastructure with.

code.description: Stable error code for programmatic matching.

message.description: Human-readable message. Safe to render directly in dashboards / terminals.

op_index.description:
Index into the JSON Patch operation array, when the error originated
from a specific op in a `PATCH /api/config` batch.

path.description:
Property path the error pertains to, when applicable. Empty when the
error is whole-config (e.g. `ReloadFailed`).

ConfigApiError.title: ConfigApiError

ConversationalAiConfig.description:
Conversational AI agent builder configuration (`[conversational_ai]` section).

**Status: Reserved for future use.** This configuration is parsed but not yet
consumed by the runtime. Setting `enabled = true` will produce a startup warning.

analytics_enabled.description: Enable conversation analytics tracking. Default: false (privacy-by-default).

auto_detect_language.description: Automatically detect user language from message content. Default: true.

default_language.description: Default language for conversations (BCP-47 tag). Default: "en".

enabled.description: Enable conversational AI features. Default: false.

escalation_confidence_threshold.description: Intent confidence below this threshold triggers escalation. Default: 0.3.

knowledge_base_tool.description: Optional tool name for RAG-based knowledge base lookup during conversations.

max_conversation_turns.description: Maximum conversation turns before auto-ending. Default: 50.

CostConfig.description: Cost tracking and budget enforcement configuration (`[cost]` section).

allow_override.description: Allow requests to exceed budget with --override flag (default: false)

enabled.description: Enable cost tracking (default: true)

enforcement.description: Cost enforcement behavior when budget limits are approached or exceeded.

warn_at_percent.description: Warn when spending reaches this percentage of limit (default: 80)

CostEnforcementConfig.description: Configuration for cost enforcement behavior when budget limits are reached.

reserve_percent.description: Reserve this percentage of budget for critical operations.

route_down_model.description: Model hint to route to when budget is exceeded (used with "route_down" mode).

catch_up_on_startup.description:
Run all overdue jobs at scheduler startup. Default: `true`.

When the machine boots late or the daemon restarts, jobs whose
`next_run` is in the past are considered "missed". With this
option enabled the scheduler fires them once before entering
the normal polling loop. Disable if you prefer missed jobs to
simply wait for their next scheduled occurrence.

enabled.description: Enable the cron subsystem. Default: `true`.

jobs.description:
Declarative cron job definitions (`[[cron.jobs]]`).

Jobs declared here are synced into the database at scheduler startup.
They use `source = "declarative"` to distinguish them from jobs
created imperatively via CLI or API. Declarative config takes
precedence on each sync: if the config changes, the DB is updated
to match. Imperative jobs are never deleted by the sync process.

max_run_history.description: Maximum number of historical cron run records to retain. Default: `50`.

CronJobDecl.description: A declarative cron job definition for the `[[cron.jobs]]` config array.

allowed_tools.description: Allowlist of tool names for agent jobs.

enabled.description: Whether the job is enabled. Default: `true`.

id.description: Stable identifier used for merge semantics across syncs.

uses_memory.description:
Whether to recall and inject memory context before this agent job runs.
Defaults to `true`; set to `false` for stateless digest jobs.

CronScheduleDecl.description: Schedule variant for declarative cron jobs.

oneOf.description: Classic cron expression.
Interval in milliseconds.
One-shot at an RFC 3339 timestamp.

health_url.description: Optional URL to check tunnel health

start_command.description:
Command template to start the tunnel. Use {port} and {host} placeholders.
Example: "bore local {port} --to bore.pub"

url_pattern.description: Optional regex to extract public URL from command stdout

DataRetentionConfig.description: Data retention and purge configuration (`[data_retention]` section).

categories.description: Limit retention enforcement to specific data categories (empty = all).

dry_run.description: Preview what would be deleted without actually removing anything.

enabled.description: Enable the `data_management` tool.

retention_days.description: Days of data to retain before purge eligibility.

DelegateAgentConfig.description: Configuration for a delegate sub-agent used by the `delegate` tool.

agentic.description: Enable agentic sub-agent mode (multi-turn tool-call loop).

agentic_timeout_secs.description:
Optional timeout in seconds for agentic sub-agent runs.
When `None`, falls back to `[delegate].agentic_timeout_secs` (default: 300).

allowed_tools.description: Allowlist of tool names available to the sub-agent in agentic mode.

max_depth.description: Max recursion depth for nested delegation

max_iterations.description: Maximum tool-call iterations in agentic mode.

memory_namespace.description:
Optional memory namespace for isolation.
When set, the sub-agent's memory operations are isolated to this namespace,
preventing cross-contamination with memory from other agents.

skills_directory.description:
Optional skills directory path (relative to workspace root) for scoped skill loading.
When unset or empty, the sub-agent falls back to the default workspace `skills/` directory.

system_prompt.description: Optional system prompt for the sub-agent

timeout_secs.description:
Optional timeout in seconds for non-agentic sub-agent provider calls.
When `None`, falls back to `[delegate].timeout_secs` (default: 120).

DelegateToolConfig.description: Global delegate tool configuration for default timeout values.

agentic_timeout_secs.description:
Default timeout in seconds for agentic sub-agent runs.
Can be overridden per-agent in `[agents.<name>]` config.
Default: 300 seconds.

timeout_secs.description:
Default timeout in seconds for non-agentic sub-agent provider calls.
Can be overridden per-agent in `[agents.<name>]` config.
Default: 120 seconds.

DeliveryConfigDecl.description: Delivery configuration for declarative cron jobs.

DingTalkConfig.description: DingTalk configuration for Stream Mode messaging

allowed_users.description: Allowed user IDs (staff IDs). Empty = deny all, "*" = allow all

client_id.description: Client ID (AppKey) from DingTalk developer console

client_secret.description: Client Secret (AppSecret) from DingTalk developer console

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

proxy_url.description:
Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).
Overrides the global `[proxy]` setting for this channel only.

allowed_users.description: Allowed Discord user IDs. Empty = deny all.

approval_timeout_secs.description: Seconds to wait for operator approval on `always_ask` tools before auto-denying.

bot_token.description: Discord bot token (from Discord Developer Portal).

draft_update_interval_ms.description:
Minimum interval (ms) between draft message edits to avoid rate limits.
Only used when `stream_mode = "partial"`.

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

guild_id.description: Optional guild (server) ID to restrict the bot to a single guild.

interrupt_on_new_message.description:
When true, a newer Discord message from the same sender in the same channel
cancels the in-flight request and starts a fresh response with preserved history.

listen_to_bots.description:
When true, process messages from other bots (not just humans).
The bot still ignores its own messages to prevent feedback loops.

mention_only.description:
When true, only respond to messages that @-mention the bot.
Other messages in the guild are silently ignored.

multi_message_delay_ms.description:
Delay (ms) between sending each message chunk in multi-message mode.
Only used when `stream_mode = "multi_message"`.

proxy_url.description:
Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).
Overrides the global `[proxy]` setting for this channel only.

stall_timeout_secs.description:
Stall-watchdog timeout in seconds. When non-zero, the bot will abort
and retry if no progress is made within this duration. 0 = disabled.

stream_mode.description:
Streaming mode for progressive response delivery.
`off` (default): single message. `partial`: editable draft updates.
`multi_message`: split response into separate messages at paragraph boundaries.

DiscordHistoryConfig.description: Discord history channel — logs ALL messages to discord.db and forwards @mentions to the agent.

allowed_users.description: Allowed Discord user IDs. Empty = allow all (open logging).

bot_token.description: Discord bot token (from Discord Developer Portal).

channel_ids.description: Discord channel IDs to watch. Empty = watch all channels.

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

guild_id.description: Optional guild (server) ID to restrict logging to a single guild.

proxy_url.description: Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).

respond_to_dms.description: When true (default), respond to @mentions in Direct Messages.

store_dms.description: When true (default), store Direct Messages in discord.db.

allowed_workspace_roots.description: Optional workspace root allowlist for Docker mount validation.

cpu_limit.description: Optional CPU limit (`None` = no explicit limit).

image.description: Runtime image used to execute shell commands.

memory_limit_mb.description: Optional memory limit in MB (`None` = no explicit limit).

DriftEntry.description:
One drift entry surfaced when in-memory `Config` diverges from the on-disk
`config.toml` (some other process — typically a hand-edit while the daemon
was stopped — wrote the file). For non-secret fields, both values are
surfaced so the dashboard can show a clean diff. For secret fields, only
the boolean `drifted` is surfaced — the secret values themselves never
leave the server.

drifted.description:
Always `true` when surfaced. Present so secret entries unambiguously
communicate the drift signal in shape `{path, secret: true, drifted: true}`.

in_memory_value.description: In-memory value (the daemon's view). Absent for secrets.

on_disk_value.description: On-disk value (what the file contains right now). Absent for secrets.

secret.description: `true` for secret fields where values cannot be exposed.

DriftEntry.title: DriftEntry

poll_interval_secs.description:
Polling interval used when the IMAP server does not advertise the IDLE
capability (RFC 2177). Ignored when IDLE is available.

EmbeddingRouteConfig.description:
Route an embedding hint to a specific provider + model.

```toml
[[embedding_routes]]
hint = "semantic"
provider = "openai"
model = "text-embedding-3-small"
dimensions = 1536

[memory]
embedding_model = "hint:semantic"
```

api_key.description: Optional API key override for this route's provider

dimensions.description: Optional embedding dimension override for this route

model.description: Embedding model to use with that provider

enabled.description: Enable emergency stop controls.

require_otp_to_resume.description: Require a valid OTP before resume operations.

state_file.description: File path used to persist estop state.

allowed_users.description: Allowed user IDs or union IDs (empty = deny all, "*" = allow all)

app_id.description: App ID from Feishu developer console

app_secret.description: App Secret from Feishu developer console

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

encrypt_key.description: Encrypt key for webhook message decryption (optional)

mention_only.description:
When true, only respond to messages that @-mention the bot in groups.
Direct messages are always processed.

port.description:
HTTP port for webhook mode only. Must be set when receive_mode = "webhook".
Not required (and ignored) for websocket mode.

proxy_url.description:
Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).
Overrides the global `[proxy]` setting for this channel only.

FirecrawlConfig.description:
Firecrawl fallback configuration for JS-heavy and bot-blocked sites.

When enabled, if the standard web fetch fails (HTTP error, empty body, or
body shorter than 100 characters suggesting a JS-only page), the tool
falls back to the Firecrawl API for stealth content extraction.

api_key_env.description: Environment variable name for the Firecrawl API key

enabled.description: Enable Firecrawl fallback

FirecrawlMode.description: Firecrawl fallback mode: scrape a single page or crawl linked pages.

oneOf.description:
Reserved for future multi-page crawl support. Accepted in config
deserialization to avoid breaking existing files, but not yet
implemented — `fetch_via_firecrawl` always uses the `/scrape` endpoint.

ca_cert_path.description: Path to the PEM-encoded CA certificate used to verify client certs.

enabled.description: Enable client certificate verification (default: false).

pinned_certs.description:
Optional SHA-256 fingerprints for certificate pinning.
When non-empty, only client certs matching one of these fingerprints are accepted.

require_client_cert.description: Reject connections that do not present a valid client certificate (default: true).

GatewayConfig.description:
Gateway server configuration (`[gateway]` section).

Controls the HTTP gateway for webhook and pairing endpoints.

allow_public_bind.description: Allow binding to non-localhost without a tunnel (default: false)

idempotency_max_keys.description: Maximum distinct idempotency keys retained in memory.

pair_rate_limit_per_minute.description: Max `/pair` requests per minute per client key.

paired_tokens.description: Paired bearer tokens (managed automatically, not user-edited)

path_prefix.description:
Optional URL path prefix for reverse-proxy deployments.
When set, all gateway routes are served under this prefix.
Must start with `/` and must not end with `/`.

rate_limit_max_keys.description: Maximum distinct client keys tracked by gateway rate limiter maps.

require_pairing.description: Require pairing before accepting requests (default: true)

session_persistence.description: Persist gateway WebSocket chat sessions to SQLite. Default: true.

session_ttl_hours.description: Auto-archive stale gateway sessions older than N hours. 0 = disabled. Default: 0.

tls.description: TLS configuration for the gateway server (`[gateway.tls]`).

trust_forwarded_headers.description:
Trust proxy-forwarded client IP headers (`X-Forwarded-For`, `X-Real-IP`).
Disabled by default; enable only behind a trusted reverse proxy.

web_dist_dir.description:
Path to the web dashboard `dist` directory.  When set, the gateway
serves the compiled frontend from the filesystem instead of requiring
it to be embedded in the binary.  Accepts absolute paths or paths
relative to the working directory.  When omitted the gateway runs in
API-only mode (no web dashboard) unless auto-detection finds it.

webhook_rate_limit_per_minute.description: Max `/webhook` requests per minute per client key.

GatewayTlsConfig.description: TLS configuration for the gateway server (`[gateway.tls]`).

cert_path.description: Path to the PEM-encoded server certificate file.

enabled.description: Enable TLS for the gateway (default: false).

key_path.description: Path to the PEM-encoded server private key file.

GeminiCliConfig.description:
Gemini CLI tool configuration (`[gemini_cli]` section).

Delegates coding tasks to the `gemini -p` CLI. Authentication uses the
binary's own session by default — no API key needed unless
`env_passthrough` includes `GOOGLE_API_KEY`.

enabled.description: Enable the `gemini_cli` tool

env_passthrough.description: Extra env vars passed to the gemini subprocess (e.g. GOOGLE_API_KEY)

max_output_bytes.description: Maximum output size in bytes (2MB default)

timeout_secs.description: Maximum execution time in seconds (coding tasks can be long)

api_key.description: API key for Google Cloud TTS.

GoogleWorkspaceAllowedOperation.description:
Google Workspace CLI (`gws`) tool configuration (`[google_workspace]` section).

## Defaults
- `enabled`: `false` (tool is not registered unless explicitly opted-in).
- `allowed_services`: empty vector, which grants access to the full default
  service set: `drive`, `sheets`, `gmail`, `calendar`, `docs`, `slides`,
  `tasks`, `people`, `chat`, `classroom`, `forms`, `keep`, `meet`, `events`.
- `credentials_path`: `None` (uses default `gws` credential discovery).
- `default_account`: `None` (uses the `gws` active account).
- `rate_limit_per_minute`: `60`.
- `timeout_secs`: `30`.
- `audit_log`: `false`.
- `credentials_path`: `None` (uses default `gws` credential discovery).
- `default_account`: `None` (uses the `gws` active account).
- `rate_limit_per_minute`: `60`.
- `timeout_secs`: `30`.
- `audit_log`: `false`.

## Compatibility
Configs that omit the `[google_workspace]` section entirely are treated as
`GoogleWorkspaceConfig::default()` (disabled, all defaults allowed). Adding
the section is purely opt-in and does not affect other config sections.

## Rollback / Migration
To revert, remove the `[google_workspace]` section from the config file (or
set `enabled = false`). No data migration is required; the tool simply stops
being registered.

methods.description: Allowed methods for the service/resource/sub_resource combination.

resource.description: Top-level resource name for the service (for example `users` for Gmail or `files` for Drive).

service.description: Google Workspace service ID (for example `gmail` or `drive`).

sub_resource.description:
Optional sub-resource for 4-segment gws commands
(for example `messages` or `drafts` under `gmail users`).
When present, the entry only matches calls that include this exact sub_resource.
When absent, the entry only matches calls with no sub_resource.

GoogleWorkspaceConfig.description:
Google Workspace CLI (`gws`) tool configuration (`[google_workspace]` section).

## Defaults
- `enabled`: `false` (tool is not registered unless explicitly opted-in).
- `allowed_services`: empty vector, which grants access to the full default
  service set: `drive`, `sheets`, `gmail`, `calendar`, `docs`, `slides`,
  `tasks`, `people`, `chat`, `classroom`, `forms`, `keep`, `meet`, `events`.
- `allowed_operations`: empty vector, which preserves the legacy behavior of
  allowing any resource/method under the allowed service set.
- `credentials_path`: `None` (uses default `gws` credential discovery).
- `default_account`: `None` (uses the `gws` active account).
- `rate_limit_per_minute`: `60`.
- `timeout_secs`: `30`.
- `audit_log`: `false`.

## Compatibility
Configs that omit the `[google_workspace]` section entirely are treated as
`GoogleWorkspaceConfig::default()` (disabled, all defaults allowed). Adding
the section is purely opt-in and does not affect other config sections.

## Rollback / Migration
To revert, remove the `[google_workspace]` section from the config file (or
set `enabled = false`). No data migration is required; the tool simply stops
being registered.

allowed_operations.description:
Restrict which resource/method combinations the agent can access.

When empty (the default), all methods under `allowed_services` remain
available for backward compatibility. When non-empty, the runtime denies
any `(service, resource, sub_resource, method)` combination that is not
explicitly listed. `sub_resource` is optional per entry: an entry without
it matches only 3-segment `gws` calls; an entry with it matches only calls
that supply that exact sub_resource value.

Each entry's `service` must appear in `allowed_services` when that list is
non-empty; config validation rejects entries that would never match at
runtime.

allowed_services.description:
Restrict which Google Workspace services the agent can access.

When empty (the default), the full default service set is allowed (see
struct-level docs). When non-empty, only the listed service IDs are
permitted. Each entry must be non-empty, lowercase alphanumeric with
optional underscores/hyphens, and unique.

audit_log.description:
Enable audit logging of every `gws` invocation (service, resource,
method, timestamp). Default: `false`.

credentials_path.description:
Path to service account JSON or OAuth client credentials file.

When `None`, the tool relies on the default `gws` credential discovery
(`gws auth login`). Set this to point at a service-account key or an
OAuth client-secrets JSON for headless / CI environments.

default_account.description:
Default Google account email to pass to `gws --account`.

When `None`, the currently active `gws` account is used.

enabled.description: Enable the `google_workspace` tool. Default: `false`.

rate_limit_per_minute.description: Maximum number of `gws` API calls allowed per minute. Default: `60`.

timeout_secs.description: Command execution timeout in seconds. Default: `30`.

HardwareConfig.description: Wizard-driven hardware configuration for physical world interaction.

baud_rate.description: Baud rate negotiated on the serial link. 115200 matches the common Arduino / ESP32 bootloader default; bump to 230400+ when your firmware explicitly supports faster rates and you need the throughput.

enabled.description: Opt in to direct physical-hardware control — GPIO pins, USB-tethered microcontrollers (Arduino, ESP32, Nucleo), or SWD/JTAG debug probes. Leave off for software-only use; turning it on without the right transport configured does nothing.

probe_target.description: Target chip identifier for `transport = probe` (e.g. `STM32F401RE`, `nRF52840_xxAA`). Passed straight to probe-rs for flash/debug operations; must match a chip probe-rs recognizes.

serial_port.description: TTY path for the `serial` transport — e.g. `/dev/ttyACM0` on Linux, `/dev/tty.usbmodem1` on macOS, `COM3` on Windows. Ignored for other transports.

transport.description: How ZeroClaw reaches the hardware: `native` = Linux SBC with direct GPIO access (Raspberry Pi, Orange Pi); `serial` = USB-tethered microcontroller speaking over a TTY; `probe` = SWD/JTAG debug probe driving a target chip via probe-rs; `none` = disabled.

workspace_datasheets.description: Index PDF schematics and datasheets from the workspace into a local RAG store, so the agent can look up pin assignments and electrical specs inline when you ask hardware questions. Off by default — turn on once the workspace has relevant PDFs dropped in.

HeartbeatConfig.description: Heartbeat configuration for periodic health pings (`[heartbeat]` section).

adaptive.description:
Enable adaptive intervals that back off on failures and speed up for
high-priority tasks. Default: `false`.

deadman_channel.description:
Channel for dead-man's switch alerts (e.g. `telegram`). Falls back to
the heartbeat delivery channel.

deadman_timeout_minutes.description:
Dead-man's switch timeout in minutes. If the heartbeat has not ticked
within this window, an alert is sent. `0` disables. Default: `0`.

deadman_to.description: Recipient for dead-man's switch alerts. Falls back to `to`.

enabled.description: Enable periodic heartbeat pings. Default: `true`.

interval_minutes.description: Interval in minutes between heartbeat pings. Minimum: `1`. Default: `30`.

load_session_context.description:
Load the channel session history before each heartbeat task execution so
the LLM has conversational context. Default: `false`.

When `true`, the session file for the configured `target`/`to` is passed
to the agent as `session_state_file`, giving it access to the recent
conversation history — just as if the user had sent a message.

max_interval_minutes.description: Maximum interval in minutes when adaptive mode backs off. Default: `120`.

max_run_history.description: Maximum number of heartbeat run history records to retain. Default: `100`.

message.description: Optional fallback task text when `HEARTBEAT.md` has no task entries.

min_interval_minutes.description: Minimum interval in minutes when adaptive mode is enabled. Default: `5`.

target.description:
Optional delivery channel for heartbeat output (for example: `telegram`).
When omitted, auto-selects the first configured channel.

task_timeout_secs.description:
Maximum wall-clock seconds allowed for a single agent invocation
(Phase 1 decision or Phase 2 task execution). `0` disables.
Default: `600` (10 minutes).

to.description:
Optional delivery recipient/chat identifier (required when `target` is
explicitly set).

two_phase.description:
Enable two-phase heartbeat: Phase 1 asks LLM whether to run, Phase 2
executes only when the LLM decides there is work to do. Saves API cost
during quiet periods. Default: `true`.

enabled.description:
Enable lifecycle hook execution.

Hooks run in-process with the same privileges as the main runtime.
Keep enabled hook handlers narrowly scoped and auditable.

HttpRequestConfig.description:
HTTP request tool configuration (`[http_request]` section).

Domain filtering: `allowed_domains` controls which hosts are reachable (use `["*"]`
for all public hosts, which is the default). If `allowed_domains` is empty, all
requests are rejected.

allow_private_hosts.description:
Allow requests to private/LAN hosts (RFC 1918, loopback, link-local, .local).
Default: false (deny private hosts for SSRF protection).

allowed_domains.description: Allowed domains for HTTP requests (exact or subdomain match)

enabled.description: Enable `http_request` tool for API interactions

max_response_size.description: Maximum response size in bytes (default: 1MB, 0 = unlimited)

allowed_contacts.description: Allowed iMessage contacts (phone numbers or email addresses). Empty = deny all.

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

IdentityConfig.description:
Identity format configuration (`[identity]` section).

Supports `"openclaw"` (default) or `"aieos"` identity documents.

aieos_inline.description: Inline AIEOS JSON (alternative to file path)

aieos_path.description: Path to AIEOS JSON file (relative to workspace)

ImageGenConfig.description:
Standalone image generation tool configuration (`[image_gen]`).

When enabled, registers an `image_gen` tool that generates images via
fal.ai's synchronous API (Flux / Nano Banana models) and saves them
to the workspace `images/` directory.

api_key_env.description: Environment variable name holding the fal.ai API key.

enabled.description: Enable the standalone image generation tool. Default: false.

api_key_env.description: Environment variable name holding the OpenAI API key.
Environment variable name holding the fal.ai API key.
Environment variable name holding the API key.

project_id_env.description: Environment variable for the Google Cloud project ID.

api_key_env.description: Environment variable name holding the API key.

section.description:
Optional section prefix to scope the init pass (e.g. `providers`).
Without it, every uninitialized nested section gets its defaults.

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

username.description: Username (defaults to nickname if not set)

JiraConfig.description:
Jira integration configuration (`[jira]`).

When `enabled = true`, registers the `jira` tool which can get tickets,
search with JQL, and add comments. Requires `base_url` and `api_token`
(or the `JIRA_API_TOKEN` env var).

## Defaults
- `enabled`: `false`
- `allowed_actions`: `["get_ticket"]` — read-only by default.
  Add `"search_tickets"` or `"comment_ticket"` to unlock them.
- `timeout_secs`: `30`

## Auth
Jira Cloud uses HTTP Basic auth: `email` + `api_token`.
`api_token` is stored encrypted at rest; set it here or via `JIRA_API_TOKEN`.

allowed_actions.description:
Actions the agent is permitted to call.
Valid values: `"get_ticket"`, `"search_tickets"`, `"comment_ticket"`.
Defaults to `["get_ticket"]` (read-only).

api_token.description: Jira API token. Encrypted at rest. Falls back to `JIRA_API_TOKEN` env var.

email.description: Jira account email used for Basic auth.

enabled.description: Enable the `jira` tool. Default: `false`.

KnowledgeConfig.description: Knowledge graph configuration for capturing and reusing expertise.

auto_capture.description: Automatically capture knowledge from conversations. Default: false.

cross_workspace_search.description: Allow searching across workspaces (disabled by default for client data isolation).

db_path.description: Path to the knowledge graph SQLite database.

enabled.description: Enable the knowledge graph tool. Default: false.

max_nodes.description: Maximum number of knowledge nodes. Default: 100000.

suggest_on_query.description: Proactively suggest relevant knowledge on queries. Default: true.

LarkConfig.description:
Lark/Feishu configuration for messaging integration.
Lark is the international version; Feishu is the Chinese version.

allowed_users.description: Allowed user IDs or union IDs (empty = deny all, "*" = allow all)

app_id.description: App ID from Lark/Feishu developer console

app_secret.description: App Secret from Lark/Feishu developer console

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

encrypt_key.description: Encrypt key for webhook message decryption (optional)

mention_only.description:
When true, only respond to messages that @-mention the bot in groups.
Direct messages are always processed.

port.description:
HTTP port for webhook mode only. Must be set when receive_mode = "webhook".
Not required (and ignored) for websocket mode.

proxy_url.description:
Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).
Overrides the global `[proxy]` setting for this channel only.

use_feishu.description: Whether to use the Feishu (Chinese) endpoint instead of Lark (International)

LarkReceiveMode.description:
How ZeroClaw receives events from Feishu / Lark.

- `websocket` (default) — persistent WSS long-connection; no public URL required.
- `webhook`             — HTTP callback server; requires a public HTTPS endpoint.

allowed_users.description:
LINE user IDs that are allowed to interact with the bot.
Used when `dm_policy = allowlist`. `["*"]` accepts everyone.

channel_access_token.description:
Long-lived channel access token (from LINE Developers Console).
Used for both the Reply API and the Push API fallback.
Falls back to the `LINE_CHANNEL_ACCESS_TOKEN` environment variable if empty.

channel_secret.description:
Channel secret (from LINE Developers Console).
Used to verify the `X-Line-Signature` header on incoming webhooks.
Falls back to the `LINE_CHANNEL_SECRET` environment variable if empty.

dm_policy.description:
DM (1:1 chat) access policy. Default: `pairing`.

- `open`      — respond to everyone
- `pairing`   — require one-time `/bind <code>` handshake on first contact
- `allowlist` — respond only to user IDs listed in `allowed_users`

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

group_policy.description:
Group / multi-person chat policy. Default: `mention`.

- `open`     — respond to every message
- `mention`  — respond only when @mentioned
- `disabled` — ignore all group messages

proxy_url.description:
Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).
Overrides the global `[proxy]` setting for this channel only.

webhook_port.description: TCP port the embedded webhook server listens on. Default: `8443`.

LineDmPolicy.description: DM (1:1 chat) access policy for the LINE channel.

oneOf.description: Respond to every DM regardless of who sent it.

Require a one-time `/bind <code>` handshake before responding (default).
ZeroClaw prints the bind code on startup; send it once to unlock access.
Respond only to LINE user IDs listed in `allowed_users`.

LineGroupPolicy.description: Group / multi-person chat policy for the LINE channel.

oneOf.description: Respond to every message in group/room chats.
Respond only when the bot is @mentioned (default).
Ignore all messages in group/room chats.

LinkEnricherConfig.description:
Automatic link understanding for inbound channel messages (`[link_enricher]`).

When enabled, URLs in incoming messages are automatically fetched and
summarised. The summary is prepended to the message before the agent
processes it, giving the LLM context about linked pages without an
explicit tool call.

enabled.description: Enable the link enricher pipeline stage (default: false)

max_links.description: Maximum number of links to fetch per message (default: 3)

LinkedInConfig.description:
LinkedIn integration configuration (`[linkedin]` section).

When enabled, the `linkedin` tool is registered in the agent tool surface.
Requires `LINKEDIN_*` credentials in the workspace `.env` file.

api_version.description: LinkedIn REST API version header (YYYYMM format).

enabled.description: Enable the LinkedIn tool.

LinkedInContentConfig.description:
Content strategy configuration for LinkedIn auto-posting (`[linkedin.content]`).

The agent reads this via the `linkedin get_content_strategy` action to know
what feeds to check, which repos to highlight, and how to write posts.

github_users.description: GitHub usernames whose public activity to reference.

instructions.description: Freeform posting instructions for the AI agent.

rss_feeds.description: RSS feed URLs to monitor for topic inspiration (titles only).

topics.description: Topics of expertise and interest for post themes.

LinkedInImageConfig.description: Image generation configuration for LinkedIn posts (`[linkedin.image]`).

card_accent_color.description: Accent color for the fallback card (CSS hex).

enabled.description: Enable image generation for posts.

fallback_card.description: Generate a branded SVG text card when all AI providers fail.

providers.description: Provider priority order. Tried in sequence; first success wins.

temp_dir.description: Temp directory for generated images, relative to workspace.

allowed_senders.description: Allowed sender handles (phone numbers) or "*" for all

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

from_phone.description: Phone number to send from (E.164 format)

signing_secret.description: Webhook signing secret for signature verification

ListEntry.description:
Single entry in the list response. Secrets carry only `path + populated`;
non-secrets additionally carry `value`.

`kind` and `type_hint` are the wire form of the field's declared
`PropKind` plus its Rust type signature. Frontends bind input renderers
to these directly (no value-sniffing). `enum_variants` is populated for
fields whose macro derive surfaces a variant list (drives `select`
option rendering).

enum_variants.description:
Variants for `enum`-kind fields — non-empty means the frontend should
render a `<select>` with these options. Empty for non-enum fields.

kind.description:
Stable kind tag — `string`, `bool`, `integer`, `float`, `enum`,
`string-array`. Lowercase-kebab so it can be used directly as a CSS
class or React key.

onboard_section.description:
Onboard section name derived from the path's first segment via
`Section::from_path`. `None` for paths that aren't part of any wizard
section. The dashboard groups list entries by this for per-section
rendering — same source the CLI wizard uses, no schema attribute.

type_hint.description:
Rust type signature, e.g. `Option<String>`, `Vec<String>`, `u64`.
Render in tooltips / hover state for the technically-curious.

drifted.description:
Properties where in-memory and on-disk values disagree. Empty when the
daemon's view matches the file. Each entry follows the `DriftEntry`
shape (secrets carry only `{path, secret: true, drifted: true}`).

LocalWhisperConfig.description:
Local/self-hosted Whisper-compatible STT endpoint (`[transcription.local_whisper]`).

Configures a self-hosted STT endpoint. Can be on localhost, a private network host, or any reachable URL.

bearer_token.description:
Bearer token for endpoint authentication.
Omit for unauthenticated local endpoints.

max_audio_bytes.description:
Maximum audio file size in bytes accepted by this endpoint.
Defaults to 25 MB — matching the cloud API cap for a safe out-of-the-box
experience. Self-hosted endpoints can accept much larger files; raise this
as needed, but note that each transcription call clones the audio buffer
into a multipart payload, so peak memory per request is ~2× this value.

timeout_secs.description: Request timeout in seconds. Defaults to 300 (large files on local GPU).

url.description: HTTP or HTTPS endpoint URL, e.g. `"http://10.10.0.1:8001/v1/transcribe"`.

access_token.description: Matrix access token for the bot account.

ack_reactions.description:
When true (default), the bot sends acknowledgement reactions while processing
(👀 on receipt, ✅ on completion). Disable to keep rooms reaction-free.

allowed_rooms.description:
Allowed Matrix room IDs or aliases. Empty = allow all rooms.
Supports canonical room IDs (`!abc:server`) and aliases (`#room:server`).

allowed_users.description: Allowed Matrix user IDs. Empty = deny all.

approval_timeout_secs.description: Seconds to wait for operator approval on `always_ask` tools before auto-denying.

draft_update_interval_ms.description: Minimum interval (ms) between draft message edits in Partial mode.

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

interrupt_on_new_message.description: Whether to interrupt an in-flight agent response when a new message arrives.

mention_only.description:
When true, only respond to messages that @-mention the bot in groups.
Direct messages are always processed.

multi_message_delay_ms.description: Delay (ms) between sending each paragraph in MultiMessage mode.

password.description: Optional login password for Matrix account (used for initial login flow).

recovery_key.description:
Optional Matrix recovery key for automatic E2EE key backup restore.
When set, ZeroClaw recovers room keys and cross-signing secrets on startup.

reply_in_thread.description:
When true (default), replies are sent as thread replies. Starts a new thread from the
incoming message when none exists. When false, only continues existing threads.

stream_mode.description:
Streaming mode for progressive response delivery.
`"off"` (default): single message. `"partial"`: edit-in-place draft.
`"multi_message"`: paragraph-split delivery.

allowed_users.description: Allowed Mattermost user IDs. Empty = deny all.

channel_id.description: Optional channel ID to restrict the bot to a single channel.

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

interrupt_on_new_message.description:
When true, a newer Mattermost message from the same sender in the same channel
cancels the in-flight request and starts a fresh response with preserved history.

mention_only.description:
When true, only respond to messages that @-mention the bot.
Other messages in the channel are silently ignored.

proxy_url.description:
Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).
Overrides the global `[proxy]` setting for this channel only.

thread_replies.description:
When true (default), replies thread on the original post.
When false, replies go to the channel root.

deferred_loading.description:
Load MCP tool schemas on-demand via `tool_search` instead of eagerly
including them in the LLM context window. When `true` (the default),
only tool names are listed in the system prompt; the LLM must call
`tool_search` to fetch full schemas before invoking a deferred tool.

enabled.description: Enable MCP tool loading.

McpServerConfig.description: Configuration for a single external MCP server.

command.description: Executable to spawn for stdio transport.

env.description: Optional environment variables for stdio transport.

headers.description: Optional HTTP headers for HTTP/SSE transports.

name.description: Display name used as a tool prefix (`<server>__<tool>`).

tool_timeout_secs.description: Optional per-call timeout in seconds (hard capped in validation).

McpTransport.description: Transport type for MCP server connections.

oneOf.description: Spawn a local process and communicate over stdin/stdout.
Connect via HTTP POST.
Connect via HTTP + Server-Sent Events.

MediaPipelineConfig.description:
Automatic media understanding pipeline configuration (`[media_pipeline]`).

When enabled, inbound channel messages with media attachments are
pre-processed before reaching the agent: audio is transcribed, images are
annotated, and videos are summarised.

describe_images.description: Add image descriptions when a vision-capable model is active.

enabled.description: Master toggle for the media pipeline (default: false).

summarize_video.description: Summarize video attachments (placeholder — requires external API).

transcribe_audio.description: Transcribe audio attachments using the configured transcription provider.

MemoryConfig.description:
Memory backend configuration (`[memory]` section).

Controls conversation memory storage, embeddings, hybrid search, response
caching, and memory snapshot/hydration. Backend-specific sub-tables
(`[memory.qdrant]`, `[memory.postgres]`) live alongside.

archive_after_days.description: Move daily/session files to the archive directory after this many days. Keeps the hot working set small without deleting history.

audit_enabled.description: Enable audit logging of memory operations.

audit_retention_days.description: Retention period for audit entries in days (default: 30).

auto_hydrate.description: Auto-hydrate from MEMORY_SNAPSHOT.md when brain.db is missing

auto_save.description: Auto-save what *you* tell ZeroClaw into memory as conversation history — the agent's own replies are not saved. Turn off if you want memory to only hold things you explicitly record via the memory tool.

backend.description: Where conversations, notes, and memories live. `sqlite` = embedded DB with optional vector + keyword hybrid search (fast, self-contained, default pick); `markdown` = plain-text files you can read and edit by hand (portable but no vector search); `lucid` = sync with the external `lucid-memory` CLI; `qdrant` = dedicated vector DB via `[memory.qdrant]` or `QDRANT_URL` env var; `none` = disable memory entirely.

chunk_max_tokens.description: Max tokens per chunk for document splitting

conflict_threshold.description: Cosine similarity threshold for conflict detection (0.0–1.0).

conversation_retention_days.description: For the sqlite backend only — drop conversation rows older than this many days to keep the DB lean. Doesn't touch core memories or notes.

embedding_cache_size.description: Max embedding cache entries before LRU eviction

embedding_dimensions.description: Vector width produced by the embedding model — must match the model's native dimension or vectors won't store correctly. Look up the number on the provider's model page.

embedding_model.description: Embedding model identifier — must match a model your chosen embedding provider serves (e.g. `text-embedding-3-small` for OpenAI). Changing this invalidates existing embeddings; you'll need to re-index.

embedding_provider.description: Source of embedding vectors for semantic search. `none` = keyword-only retrieval (no API calls, no vector cost); `openai` = OpenAI's embedding API; `custom:URL` = any OpenAI-compatible embedding endpoint (LiteLLM, local gateway, etc.).

fts_early_return_score.description: FTS score above which to early-return without vector search (0.0–1.0).

hygiene_enabled.description: Run the periodic hygiene pass that archives stale daily/session files and enforces retention windows. Leave on unless you want to manage cleanup yourself.

keyword_weight.description: How heavily BM25 (keyword) overlap counts when `search_mode = hybrid`. Raise toward 1.0 for exact-term matching; lower it when paraphrases should still score well.

min_relevance_score.description:
Minimum hybrid score (0.0–1.0) for a memory to be included in context.
Memories scoring below this threshold are dropped to prevent irrelevant
context from bleeding into conversations. Default: 0.4

postgres.description:
Configuration for PostgreSQL memory backend (`[memory.postgres]`).
Only used when `backend = "postgres"`.

purge_after_days.description: Delete archived files permanently after this many days. Set high if you need long-term history; set low for privacy / disk-space reasons.

qdrant.description:
Configuration for Qdrant vector database backend.
Only used when `backend = "qdrant"`.

rerank_enabled.description: Enable LLM reranking when candidate count exceeds threshold.

rerank_threshold.description: Minimum candidate count to trigger reranking.

response_cache_enabled.description: Enable LLM response caching to avoid paying for duplicate prompts

response_cache_hot_entries.description: Max in-memory hot cache entries for the two-tier response cache (default: 256)

response_cache_max_entries.description: Max number of cached responses before LRU eviction (default: 5000)

response_cache_ttl_minutes.description: TTL in minutes for cached responses (default: 60)

retrieval_stages.description: Retrieval stages to execute in order. Valid: "cache", "fts", "vector".

search_mode.description: How memories are retrieved: `bm25` = keyword-only (no embeddings, cheapest); `embedding` = vector similarity only (needs an embedding provider); `hybrid` = blended keyword + vector score using the weights above (most robust).

snapshot_enabled.description: Enable periodic export of core memories to MEMORY_SNAPSHOT.md

sqlite_open_timeout_secs.description:
For sqlite backend: max seconds to wait when opening the DB (e.g. file locked).
None = wait indefinitely (default). Recommended max: 300.

vector_weight.description: How heavily vector (semantic) similarity counts when `search_mode = hybrid`. Raise toward 1.0 to favor meaning-based matches; lower it to lean on keyword overlap instead.

read_only_namespaces.description: Namespaces that are read-only (writes are rejected).

retention_days_by_category.description: Retention days by category (overrides global). Keys: "core", "daily", "conversation".

Microsoft365Config.description:
Microsoft 365 integration via Microsoft Graph API (`[microsoft365]` section).

Provides access to Outlook mail, Teams messages, Calendar events,
OneDrive files, and SharePoint search.

client_secret.description: Azure AD client secret (stored encrypted when secrets.encrypt = true)

enabled.description: Enable Microsoft 365 integration

token_cache_encrypted.description: Encrypt the token cache file on disk

user_id.description: User principal name or "me" (for delegated flows)

backup_path.description:
Backup path written when migration ran; absent when the config was
already at the current schema version.

MochatConfig.description: Mochat channel configuration (Mochat customer service API)

allowed_users.description: Allowed user IDs. Empty = deny all, "*" = allow all

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

poll_interval_secs.description: Poll interval in seconds for new messages. Default: 5

api_key.description: Secret API token for this provider — grab it from the provider's dashboard (OpenAI platform, Anthropic console, OpenRouter keys page, etc.). Stored via the OS keyring when possible; never commit it to config.toml directly.

api_path.description: Path suffix appended to the base URL. Almost no one needs this — only touch it for custom reverse-proxy routing where your gateway mounts the API under a non-standard prefix.

azure_openai_api_version.description: Azure OpenAI API version string (e.g. `2024-10-21`). Azure-only; must match a version your resource supports. Ignore for other providers.

azure_openai_deployment.description: Azure OpenAI deployment name — the deployment you created in Azure AI Studio that wraps a specific model. Azure-only; ignore for other providers.

azure_openai_resource.description: Azure OpenAI resource name (the `<resource>` part of `<resource>.openai.azure.com`). Azure-only; ignore for OpenAI, Anthropic, and everything else.

base_url.description: HTTPS endpoint the client hits. Override when pointing at a self-hosted gateway (LiteLLM, vLLM, Ollama), a regional endpoint, or a proxy; leave unset to use the provider's public endpoint.

extra_headers.description: Extra HTTP headers sent with every request. Niche — used for auth bridges, corporate proxies, or custom gateways that demand a tracing header. Most users never touch this; edit `config.toml` directly if you need it.

max_tokens.description: Hard cap on response length in tokens. Most models enforce sensible built-in limits already — leave unset unless you specifically need to clip long outputs for cost or latency reasons.

merge_system_into_user.description: Provider-specific quirk: fold the system prompt into the first user message instead of sending a separate system role. Only needed for models that reject (or mishandle) a standalone system role — e.g. certain older Mistral variants.

model.description: Model identifier to send with each request — the ID string from the provider's catalog (e.g. `gpt-4o`, `claude-sonnet-4-5`, `llama-3.3-70b`). Must match a model the provider actually serves on this account.

name.description: Override the provider type label. Rarely needed — only useful when you run two profiles against the same provider type (e.g. two different OpenAI-compatible gateways) and want to tell them apart in logs.

provider_extra.description:
Extra JSON parameters to include in API requests.
Merged at the top level of the request body, allowing provider-specific
features (routing, transforms, etc.) without code changes.
Example: `provider_extra = { provider = { only = ["Anthropic"] } }`

requires_openai_auth.description: When true, the client pulls credentials from `OPENAI_API_KEY` or `~/.codex/auth.json` instead of the `api_key` field above. Turn on only for the OpenAI Codex provider; leave off for standard API-key providers.

temperature.description:
Sampling temperature passed to the model. Lower values (0.0–0.3) give
deterministic, near-verbatim output — fits code, routing, summarization.
Higher values (0.7–1.2) give more varied output — fits open-ended chat.

timeout_secs.description: HTTP request timeout in seconds. Bump this for slow local providers (Ollama on CPU, big local models) or high-latency networks; leave unset otherwise.

wire_api.description: Wire protocol flavor: `"responses"` for OpenAI's Codex/Responses API, `"chat_completions"` for everything else (OpenAI chat, Anthropic, OpenRouter, Groq, local gateways). Auto-selected per provider — only override if you're forcing an unusual combination.

ModelRouteConfig.description:
Route a task hint to a specific provider + model.

```toml
[[model_routes]]
hint = "reasoning"
provider = "openrouter"
model = "anthropic/claude-opus-4-20250514"

[[model_routes]]
hint = "fast"
provider = "groq"
model = "llama-3.3-70b-versatile"
```

Usage: pass `hint:reasoning` as the model parameter to route the request.

api_key.description: Optional API key override for this route's provider

model.description: Model to use with that provider

provider.description: Provider to route to (must match a known provider name)

MqttConfig.description:
MQTT channel configuration (SOP listener).

Subscribes to MQTT topics and dispatches incoming messages
to the SOP engine for processing.

broker_url.description:
MQTT broker URL (e.g., `mqtt://localhost:1883` or `mqtts://broker.example.com:8883`).
Use `mqtt://` for plain connections or `mqtts://` for TLS.

client_id.description: MQTT client ID (must be unique per broker).

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

keep_alive_secs.description: Keep-alive interval in seconds (default: 30). Prevents broker disconnect on idle.

qos.description: MQTT QoS level (0 = at-most-once, 1 = at-least-once, 2 = exactly-once). Default: 1.

topics.description:
Topics to subscribe to (e.g., `sensors/#`, `alerts/+/critical`).
At least one topic is required.

use_tls.description:
Enable TLS encryption. Must match the broker_url scheme:
- `mqtt://` → `use_tls: false`
- `mqtts://` → `use_tls: true`

allow_remote_fetch.description: Allow fetching remote image URLs (http/https). Disabled by default.

max_image_size_mb.description: Maximum image payload size in MiB before base64 encoding.

max_images.description: Maximum number of image attachments accepted per request.

vision_model.description:
Model to use when routing to the vision provider (e.g. `"llava:7b"`).
Only used when `vision_provider` is set.

vision_provider.description:
Provider name to use for vision/image messages (e.g. `"ollama"`).
When set, messages containing `[IMAGE:]` markers are routed to this
provider instead of the default text provider.

NevisConfig.description:
Nevis IAM integration configuration.

When `enabled` is true, ZeroClaw validates incoming requests against a Nevis
Security Suite instance and maps Nevis roles to tool/workspace permissions.

client_id.description: OAuth2 client ID registered in Nevis.

client_secret.description: OAuth2 client secret. Encrypted via SecretStore when stored on disk.

enabled.description: Enable Nevis IAM integration. Defaults to false for backward compatibility.

instance_url.description: Base URL of the Nevis instance (e.g. `https://nevis.example.com`).

jwks_url.description: JWKS endpoint URL for local token validation.

require_mfa.description: Require MFA verification for all Nevis-authenticated requests.

role_mapping.description: Nevis role to ZeroClaw permission mappings.

NevisRoleMappingConfig.description: Maps a Nevis role to ZeroClaw tool permissions and workspace access.

workspace_access.description: Workspace names this role can access. Use `"all"` for unrestricted.

zeroclaw_permissions.description: Tool names this role can access. Use `"all"` for unrestricted tool access.

NextcloudTalkConfig.description: Nextcloud Talk bot configuration (webhook receive + OCS send API).

allowed_users.description: Allowed Nextcloud actor IDs (`[]` = deny all, `"*"` = allow all).

app_token.description: Bot app token used for OCS API bearer auth.

bot_name.description:
Display name of the bot in Nextcloud Talk (e.g. "zeroclaw").
Used to filter out the bot's own messages and prevent feedback loops.
If not set, defaults to an empty string (no self-message filtering by name).

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

proxy_url.description:
Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).
Overrides the global `[proxy]` setting for this channel only.

webhook_secret.description:
Shared secret for webhook signature verification.

Can also be set via `ZEROCLAW_NEXTCLOUD_TALK_WEBHOOK_SECRET`.

NodeTransportConfig.description: Secure transport configuration for inter-node communication (`[node_transport]`).

connection_pool_size.description: Maximum number of connections per peer.

enabled.description: Enable the secure transport layer.

max_request_age_secs.description: Maximum age of signed requests in seconds (replay protection).

require_https.description: Require HTTPS for all node communication.

shared_secret.description: Shared secret for HMAC authentication between nodes.

tls_key_path.description: Path to TLS private key file.

NodesConfig.description:
Configuration for the dynamic node discovery system (`[nodes]`).

When enabled, external processes/devices can connect via WebSocket
at `/ws/nodes` and advertise their capabilities at runtime.

auth_token.description: Optional bearer token for node authentication.

enabled.description: Enable dynamic node discovery endpoint.

max_nodes.description: Maximum number of concurrent node connections.

NotionConfig.description:
Notion integration configuration (`[notion]`).

When `enabled = true`, the agent polls a Notion database for pending tasks
and exposes a `notion` tool for querying, reading, creating, and updating pages.
Requires `api_key` (or the `NOTION_API_KEY` env var) and `database_id`.

otel_endpoint.description: OTLP endpoint (e.g. `"http://localhost:4318"`). Only used when backend = `"otel"`.

otel_headers.description:
Optional HTTP headers sent with every OTLP export request (e.g. authorization).
Specified as key-value pairs in TOML:
```toml
[observability.otel_headers]
Authorization = "Bearer sk-..."
```

otel_service_name.description: Service name reported to the OTel collector. Defaults to "zeroclaw".

runtime_trace_mode.description:
Runtime trace storage mode: "none" | "rolling" | "full".
Controls whether model replies and tool-call diagnostics are persisted.

runtime_trace_path.description: Runtime trace file path. Relative paths are resolved under workspace_dir.

OnboardStateConfig.description:
Multi-client workspace isolation configuration.

When enabled, each client engagement gets an isolated workspace with
separate memory, audit, secrets, and tool restrictions.
Opaque state the `zeroclaw onboard` flow writes so it can tell, on a
re-run, which sections the user has already walked through at least
once — which lets it offer "Reconfigure? [y/N]" skip gates instead of
forcing users through every field again.

This is meta-state about the onboard process, not user-facing config.

completed_sections.description:
Section keys the user has completed at least once via onboard.
Values are the lowercased Section variant names
(`"workspace"`, `"providers"`, …).

api_key.description: OpenAI API key for Whisper transcription.

OpenCodeCliConfig.description:
OpenCode CLI tool configuration (`[opencode_cli]` section).

Delegates coding tasks to the `opencode run` CLI. Authentication uses the
binary's own session by default — no API key needed unless
`env_passthrough` includes provider-specific keys.

enabled.description: Enable the `opencode_cli` tool

env_passthrough.description: Extra env vars passed to the opencode subprocess

max_output_bytes.description: Maximum output size in bytes (2MB default)

timeout_secs.description: Maximum execution time in seconds (coding tasks can be long)

OpenVpnTunnelConfig.description:
OpenVPN tunnel configuration (`[tunnel.openvpn]`).

Required when `tunnel.provider = "openvpn"`. Omitting this section entirely
preserves previous behavior. Setting `tunnel.provider = "none"` (or removing
the `[tunnel.openvpn]` block) cleanly reverts to no-tunnel mode.

Defaults: `connect_timeout_secs = 30`.

advertise_address.description:
Advertised address once VPN is connected (e.g., `"10.8.0.2:42617"`).
When omitted the tunnel falls back to `http://{local_host}:{local_port}`.

auth_file.description: Optional path to auth credentials file (`--auth-user-pass`).

config_file.description: Path to `.ovpn` configuration file (must not be empty).

connect_timeout_secs.description: Connection timeout in seconds (default: 30, must be > 0).

extra_args.description: Extra openvpn CLI arguments forwarded verbatim.

cache_valid_secs.description: Reuse window for recently validated OTP codes.

challenge_max_attempts.description: Maximum number of OTP challenge attempts before lockout.

enabled.description: Enable OTP gating. Defaults to disabled for backward compatibility.

gated_domains.description: Explicit domain patterns gated by OTP.

oneOf.description: Time-based one-time password (RFC 6238).
Future method for paired-device confirmations.
Future method for local CLI challenge prompts.

PacingConfig.description:
Pacing controls for slow/local LLM workloads (`[pacing]` section).

All fields are optional and default to values that preserve existing
behavior. When set, they extend — not replace — the existing timeout
and loop-detection subsystems.

loop_detection_enabled.description:
Enable pattern-based loop detection (exact repeat, ping-pong,
no-progress). Defaults to `true`.

loop_detection_max_repeats.description:
Number of consecutive identical tool+args calls before the first
escalation (Warning). Defaults to 3.

loop_detection_min_elapsed_secs.description:
Minimum elapsed seconds before loop detection activates.
Tasks completing under this threshold get aggressive loop protection;
longer-running tasks receive a grace period before the detector starts
counting. `None` means loop detection is always active (existing behavior).

loop_detection_window_size.description:
Sliding window size for the pattern-based loop detector.
Defaults to 20.

loop_ignore_tools.description:
Tool names excluded from identical-output / alternating-pattern loop
detection. Useful for browser workflows where `browser_screenshot`
structurally resembles a loop even when making progress.

message_timeout_scale_max.description:
Override for the hardcoded timeout scaling cap (default: 4).
The channel message timeout budget is computed as:
  `message_timeout_secs * min(max_tool_iterations, message_timeout_scale_max)`
Raising this value lets long multi-step tasks with slow local models
receive a proportionally larger budget without inflating the base timeout.

step_timeout_secs.description:
Per-step timeout in seconds: the maximum time allowed for a single
LLM inference turn, independent of the total message budget.
`None` means no per-step timeout (existing behavior).

code_ttl_secs.description: Time-to-live for pending pairing codes in seconds (default: 3600)

lockout_secs.description: Lockout duration in seconds after max attempts (default: 300)

max_failed_attempts.description: Maximum failed pairing attempts before lockout (default: 5)

PatchOp.description:
One JSON Patch (RFC 6902) operation. We support a strict subset:
`add`, `remove`, `replace`, `test`. `move` and `copy` are explicitly
rejected at apply time with `op_not_supported` because safe reference-
graph rewriting isn't part of this PR.

`comment` is a ZeroClaw extension — when provided it accompanies the
resulting TOML write so future maintainers can see why a value was set.
Honored once the comment-preserving write path is wired through (step 7);
accepted here so the API shape doesn't churn.
PatchOp.title: PatchOp

PatchOpResult.description: Single result entry in a successful PATCH response, one per applied op.

comment.description:
Comment that was applied alongside this op (if any). Echoed so
clients can confirm the comment was actually written to disk
without having to round-trip through `GET` and parse the TOML.

value.description:
The resulting value at the target path after the op applied.
