`None` for secret paths (per the secrets-handling boundary), and for
`remove` ops where the field was reset to its default.

PeripheralBoardConfig.description: Configuration for a single peripheral board (e.g. STM32, RPi GPIO).

PeripheralsConfig.description:
Peripheral board integration configuration (`[peripherals]` section).

Boards become agent tools when enabled.

datasheet_dir.description:
Path to datasheet docs (relative to workspace) for RAG retrieval.
Place .md/.txt files named by board (e.g. nucleo-f401re.md, rpi-gpio.md).

enabled.description: Enable peripheral support (boards become agent tools)

region.description: Server region: `"us"` (USA), `"eu"` (Europe), `"ap"` (Asia), `"br"` (South America), `"au"` (Australia), or omit for auto.

token.description: Pinggy access token (optional — free tier works without one).

allowed_tools.description:
Tools allowed in pipeline steps. Steps referencing tools not on this
list are rejected before execution.

enabled.description:
Enable the `execute_pipeline` meta-tool.
Default: `false`.

max_steps.description:
Maximum number of steps allowed in a single pipeline invocation.
Default: `20`.

PiperTtsConfig.description: Piper TTS provider configuration (local GPU-accelerated, OpenAI-compatible endpoint).

api_url.description: Base URL for the Piper TTS HTTP server (e.g. `"http://127.0.0.1:5000/v1/audio/speech"`).

PluginSecurityConfig.description:
Plugin signature verification configuration (`[plugins.security]`).

Controls Ed25519 signature verification for plugin manifests.
In `strict` mode, only plugins signed by a trusted publisher key are loaded.
In `permissive` mode, unsigned or untrusted plugins produce warnings but are
still loaded. In `disabled` mode (the default), no signature checking occurs.

trusted_publisher_keys.description: Hex-encoded Ed25519 public keys of trusted plugin publishers.

auto_discover.description: Auto-discover and load plugins on startup

enabled.description: Enable the plugin system (default: false)

max_plugins.description: Maximum number of plugins that can be loaded

PostgresMemoryConfig.description:
PostgreSQL memory backend configuration (`[memory.postgres]` section).

Used when `[memory].backend = "postgres"`. Connection parameters
(`db_url`, `schema`, `table`, `connect_timeout_secs`) live under
`[storage.provider.config]`; this struct only holds vector-search settings.

vector_enabled.description: Enable pgvector extension for hybrid vector+keyword recall.

default_language.description: Default report language (en, de, fr, it). Default: "en".

enabled.description: Enable the project_intel tool. Default: false.

include_git_data.description: Include git log data in reports. Default: true.

include_jira_data.description: Include Jira data in reports. Default: false.

jira_base_url.description: Jira instance base URL (required if include_jira_data is true).

risk_sensitivity.description: Risk detection sensitivity: low, medium, high. Default: "medium".

PropPutBody.description:
PUT body. Value is `serde_json::Value` so typed values (booleans, arrays,
numbers) round-trip correctly without going through the CLI's
comma-delimited string parser.
PropPutBody.title: PropPutBody

ProvidersConfig.description:
Top-level `[providers]` section. Wraps model provider profiles, routing rules,
and an optional fallback reference.

embedding_routes.description: Embedding routing rules — route `hint:<name>` to specific provider+model combos.

fallback.description:
Key of the provider entry to use when no route matches.
Optional — if unset, requests without a matching route fail at runtime.

model_routes.description: Model routing rules — route `hint:<name>` to specific provider+model combos.

models.description: Named model provider profiles keyed by id.

ProxyConfig.description: Proxy configuration for outbound HTTP/HTTPS/SOCKS5 traffic (`[proxy]` section).

all_proxy.description: Fallback proxy URL for all schemes.

enabled.description: Enable proxy support for selected scope.

http_proxy.description: Proxy URL for HTTP requests (supports http, https, socks5, socks5h).

https_proxy.description: Proxy URL for HTTPS requests (supports http, https, socks5, socks5h).

no_proxy.description: No-proxy bypass list. Same format as NO_PROXY.

ProxyScope.description: Proxy application scope — determines which outbound traffic uses the proxy.

oneOf.description: Use system environment proxy variables only.
Apply proxy to all ZeroClaw-managed HTTP traffic (default).
Apply proxy only to explicitly listed service selectors.

QQConfig.description: QQ Official Bot configuration (Tencent QQ Bot SDK)

allowed_users.description: Allowed user IDs. Empty = deny all, "*" = allow all

app_id.description: App ID from QQ Bot developer console

app_secret.description: App Secret from QQ Bot developer console

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

proxy_url.description:
Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).
Overrides the global `[proxy]` setting for this channel only.

QdrantConfig.description:
Qdrant vector database backend configuration (`[memory.qdrant]` section).

Used when `[memory].backend = "qdrant"`. URL, collection, and API key all
fall back to environment variables (`QDRANT_URL`, `QDRANT_COLLECTION`,
`QDRANT_API_KEY`) when not set explicitly.

api_key.description:
Optional API key for Qdrant Cloud or secured instances.
Falls back to `QDRANT_API_KEY` env var if not set.

collection.description:
Qdrant collection name for storing memories.
Falls back to `QDRANT_COLLECTION` env var, or default "zeroclaw_memories".

url.description:
Qdrant server URL (e.g. `"http://localhost:6333"`).
Falls back to `QDRANT_URL` env var if not set.

QueryClassificationConfig.description:
Automatic query classification — classifies user messages by keyword/pattern
and routes to the appropriate model hint. Disabled by default.

enabled.description: Enable automatic query classification. Default: `false`.

rules.description: Classification rules evaluated in priority order.

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

refresh_token.description: Reddit OAuth2 refresh token for persistent access.

subreddit.description:
Optional subreddit to filter messages (without `r/` prefix).
When set, only messages from this subreddit are processed.

ReliabilityConfig.description:
Reliability and supervision configuration (`[reliability]` section).

Controls provider retries, fallback chains, API key rotation, and channel restart backoff.

api_keys.description:
Additional API keys for round-robin rotation on rate-limit (429) errors.
The primary `api_key` is always tried first; these are extras.

model_fallbacks.description:
Per-model fallback chains. When a model fails, try these alternatives in order.
Example: `{ "claude-opus-4-20250514" = ["claude-sonnet-4-20250514", "gpt-4o"] }`

provider_backoff_ms.description: Base backoff (ms) for provider retry delay.

provider_retries.description: Retries per provider before failing over.

scheduler_retries.description: Max retries for cron job execution attempts.

max_cpu_time_seconds.description: Maximum CPU time in seconds per command

max_memory_mb.description: Maximum memory in MB per command

reasoning_effort.description: Optional reasoning effort for providers that expose a level control.

reasoning_enabled.description:
Global reasoning override for providers that expose explicit controls.
- `None`: provider default behavior
- `Some(true)`: request reasoning/thinking when supported
- `Some(false)`: disable reasoning/thinking when supported

oneOf.description: Auto-detect best available (default)
Landlock (Linux kernel LSM, native)
Firejail (user-space sandbox)
Bubblewrap (user namespaces)
Docker container isolation
macOS sandbox-exec (Seatbelt)
No sandboxing (application-layer only)

enabled.description: Enable sandboxing (None = auto-detect, Some = explicit)

SchedulerConfig.description: Scheduler configuration for periodic task execution (`[scheduler]` section).

enabled.description: Enable the built-in scheduler loop.

max_concurrent.description: Maximum tasks executed per scheduler polling cycle.

max_tasks.description: Maximum number of persisted scheduled tasks.

oneOf.description: Pure keyword search (FTS5 BM25)
Pure vector/semantic search
Weighted combination of keyword + vector (default)

SecretResponse.title: SecretResponse

encrypt.description: Enable encryption for API keys and tokens in config.toml

SecurityConfig.description: Security configuration for sandboxing, resource limits, and audit logging

nevis.description: Nevis IAM integration for SSO/MFA authentication and role-based access.

otp.description: OTP gating configuration for sensitive actions/domains.

webauthn.description: WebAuthn / FIDO2 hardware key authentication configuration.

SecurityOpsConfig.description: Managed Cybersecurity Service (MCSS) dashboard agent configuration (`[security_ops]`).

auto_triage.description: Automatically triage incoming alerts without user prompt.

enabled.description: Enable security operations tools.

max_auto_severity.description:
Maximum severity level that can be auto-remediated without approval.
One of: "low", "medium", "high", "critical". Default: "low".

playbooks_dir.description: Directory containing incident response playbook definitions (JSON).

require_approval_for_actions.description: Require human approval before executing playbook actions.

siem_integration.description: Optional SIEM webhook URL for alert ingestion.

ShellToolConfig.description:
Shell tool configuration (`[shell_tool]` section).

Controls the behaviour of the `shell` execution tool. The main
tunable is `timeout_secs` — the maximum wall-clock time a single
shell command may run before it is killed.

timeout_secs.description: Maximum shell command execution time in seconds (default: 60).

account.description: E.164 phone number of the signal-cli account (e.g. "+1234567890").

allowed_from.description: Allowed sender phone numbers (E.164) or "*" for all.

approval_timeout_secs.description: Seconds to wait for operator approval on `always_ask` tools before auto-denying.

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

group_id.description:
Optional group ID to filter messages.
- `None` or omitted: accept all messages (DMs and groups)
- `"dm"`: only accept direct messages
- Specific group ID: only accept messages from that group

http_url.description: Base URL for the signal-cli HTTP daemon (e.g. `"http://127.0.0.1:8686"`).

ignore_attachments.description: Skip messages that are attachment-only (no text body).

proxy_url.description:
Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).
Overrides the global `[proxy]` setting for this channel only.

enabled.description:
Enable automatic skill creation after successful multi-step tasks.
Default: `false`.

max_skills.description:
Maximum number of auto-generated skills to keep.
When exceeded, the oldest auto-generated skill is removed (LRU eviction).

similarity_threshold.description:
Embedding similarity threshold for deduplication.
Skills with descriptions more similar than this value are skipped.

cooldown_secs.description:
Minimum interval (in seconds) between improvements for the same skill.
Default: `3600` (1 hour).

enabled.description:
Enable automatic skill improvement after successful skill usage.
Default: `true`.

allow_scripts.description:
Allow script-like files in skills (`.sh`, `.bash`, `.ps1`, shebang shell files).
Default: `false` (secure by default).

open_skills_dir.description:
Optional path to a local open-skills repository.
If unset, defaults to `$HOME/open-skills` when enabled.

open_skills_enabled.description:
Enable loading and syncing the community open-skills repository.
Default: `false` (opt-in).

prompt_injection_mode.description:
Controls how skills are injected into the system prompt.
`full` preserves legacy behavior. `compact` keeps context small and loads skills on demand.

registry_url.description:
URL of the skills registry repository for bare-name installs.
Default: `https://github.com/zeroclaw-labs/zeroclaw-skills`

skill_creation.description: Autonomous skill creation from successful multi-step task executions.

skill_improvement.description: Automatic skill self-improvement after successful skill usage.

oneOf.description: Inline full skill instructions and tool metadata into the system prompt.
Inline only compact skill metadata (name/description/location) and load details on demand.

allowed_users.description: Allowed Slack user IDs. Empty = deny all.

app_token.description: Slack app-level token for Socket Mode (xapp-...).

approval_timeout_secs.description: Seconds to wait for operator approval on `always_ask` tools before auto-denying.

cancel_reaction.description:
Emoji reaction name (without colons) that cancels an in-flight request.
For example, `"x"` means reacting with `:x:` cancels the task.
Leave unset to disable reaction-based cancellation.

channel_ids.description:
Explicit list of channel IDs to watch.
Empty = listen across all accessible channels.
Migrated from the legacy `channel_id` singular field.

draft_update_interval_ms.description: Minimum interval (ms) between draft message edits to avoid Slack rate limits.

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

interrupt_on_new_message.description:
When true, a newer Slack message from the same sender in the same channel
cancels the in-flight request and starts a fresh response with preserved history.

mention_only.description:
When true, only respond to messages that @-mention the bot in groups.
Direct messages remain allowed.

proxy_url.description:
Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).
Overrides the global `[proxy]` setting for this channel only.

stream_drafts.description: Enable progressive draft message streaming via `chat.update`.

thread_replies.description:
When true (default), replies stay in the originating Slack thread.
When false, replies go to the channel root instead.

use_markdown_blocks.description:
Use the newer Slack `markdown` block type (12 000 char limit, richer formatting).
Defaults to false (uses universally supported `section` blocks with `mrkdwn`).
Enable this only if your Slack workspace supports the `markdown` block type.

SopConfig.description:
Standard Operating Procedures engine configuration (`[sop]`).

The `default_execution_mode` field uses the `SopExecutionMode` type from
`sop::types` (re-exported via `sop::SopExecutionMode`). To avoid circular
module references, config stores it using the same enum definition.

approval_timeout_secs.description:
Approval timeout in seconds. When a run waits for approval longer than
this, Critical/High-priority SOPs auto-approve; others stay waiting.
Set to 0 to disable timeout.

default_execution_mode.description:
Default execution mode for SOPs that omit `execution_mode`.
Values: `auto`, `supervised` (default), `step_by_step`,
`priority_based`, `deterministic`.

max_concurrent_total.description: Maximum total concurrent SOP runs across all SOPs.

max_finished_runs.description:
Maximum number of finished runs kept in memory for status queries.
Oldest runs are evicted when over capacity. 0 = unlimited.

sops_dir.description:
Directory containing SOP definitions (subdirs with SOP.toml + SOP.md).
Falls back to `<workspace>/sops` when omitted.

StorageProviderConfig.description: Storage provider backend configuration for remote storage backends.

connect_timeout_secs.description: Optional connection timeout in seconds for remote providers.

db_url.description:
Connection URL for remote providers.
Accepts legacy aliases: dbURL, database_url, databaseUrl.

StorageProviderSection.description: Wrapper for the storage provider configuration section.

StreamMode.description: Streaming mode for channels that support progressive message updates.

oneOf.description: No streaming -- send the complete response as a single message (default).
Update a draft message with every flush interval.
Send the response as multiple separate messages at paragraph boundaries.

SwarmConfig.description: Configuration for a swarm of coordinated agents.

agents.description: Ordered list of agent names (must reference keys in `agents`).

description.description: Optional description shown to the LLM when choosing swarms.

router_prompt.description: System prompt for router strategy (used to pick the best agent).

timeout_secs.description: Maximum total timeout for the swarm execution in seconds.

SwarmStrategy.description: Orchestration strategy for a swarm of agents.

oneOf.description: Run agents sequentially; each agent's output feeds into the next.
Run agents in parallel; collect all outputs.
Use the LLM to pick the best agent for the task.

funnel.description: Use Tailscale Funnel (public internet) vs Serve (tailnet only)

ack_reactions.description:
Override for the top-level `ack_reactions` setting. When `None`, the
channel falls back to `[channels].ack_reactions`. When set
explicitly, it takes precedence.

allowed_users.description: Allowed Telegram user IDs or usernames. Empty = deny all.

approval_timeout_secs.description:
How long (seconds) to wait for the operator to tap an inline-keyboard
button on a tool approval prompt before auto-denying. Default: 120.

draft_update_interval_ms.description: Minimum interval (ms) between draft message edits to avoid rate limits.

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

interrupt_on_new_message.description:
When true, a newer Telegram message from the same sender in the same chat
cancels the in-flight request and starts a fresh response with preserved history.

mention_only.description:
When true, only respond to messages that @-mention the bot in groups.
Direct messages are always processed.

proxy_url.description:
Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).
Overrides the global `[proxy]` setting for this channel only.

stream_mode.description: Streaming mode for progressive response delivery via message edits.

TextBrowserConfig.description:
Text browser tool configuration (`[text_browser]` section).

Uses text-based browsers (lynx, links, w3m) to render web pages as plain
text. Designed for headless/SSH environments without graphical browsers.

enabled.description: Enable `text_browser` tool

preferred_browser.description: Preferred text browser ("lynx", "links", or "w3m"). If unset, auto-detects.

ThinkingLevel.description: How deeply the model should reason for a given message.

ToolFilterGroup.description:
A named group of MCP tool patterns with an activation mode.

Each group lists glob patterns for MCP tool names (prefix `mcp_`) and an
optional set of keywords that trigger inclusion in `dynamic` mode.
Built-in (non-MCP) tools always pass through and are never affected by
`tool_filter_groups`.

# Example
```toml
[[agent.tool_filter_groups]]
mode = "always"
tools = ["mcp_filesystem_*"]
keywords = []

[[agent.tool_filter_groups]]
mode = "dynamic"
tools = ["mcp_browser_*"]
keywords = ["browse", "website", "url", "search"]
```

filter_builtins.description: When true, also filter built-in tools (not just MCP tools).

keywords.description:
Keywords that activate this group in `dynamic` mode (case-insensitive substring).
Ignored when `mode = "always"`.

tools.description: Glob patterns matching MCP tool names (single `*` wildcard supported).

oneOf.description: Tools in this group are always included in every turn.

Tools in this group are included only when the user message contains
at least one of the configured `keywords` (case-insensitive substring match).

TranscriptionConfig.description:
Voice transcription configuration with multi-provider support.

The top-level `api_url`, `model`, and `api_key` fields remain for backward
compatibility with existing Groq-based configurations.

api_key.description:
API key used for transcription requests (Groq provider).

If unset, runtime falls back to `GROQ_API_KEY` for backward compatibility.

enabled.description: Enable voice transcription for channels that support it.

initial_prompt.description:
Optional initial prompt to bias transcription toward expected vocabulary
(proper nouns, technical terms, etc.). Sent as the `prompt` field in the
Whisper API request.

language.description: Optional language hint (ISO-639-1, e.g. "en", "ru") for Groq provider.

max_duration_secs.description: Maximum voice duration in seconds (messages longer than this are skipped).

transcribe_non_ptt_audio.description:
Also transcribe non-PTT (forwarded/regular) audio messages on WhatsApp,
not just voice notes.  Default: `false` (preserves legacy behavior).

default_voice.description: Default voice ID passed to the selected provider.

enabled.description: Enable TTS synthesis.

max_text_length.description: Maximum input text length in characters (default 4096).

TunnelConfig.description:
Tunnel configuration for exposing the gateway publicly (`[tunnel]` section).

Supported providers: `"none"` (default), `"cloudflare"`, `"tailscale"`, `"ngrok"`, `"openvpn"`, `"pinggy"`, `"custom"`.

provider.description: How the gateway gets exposed to the public internet so webhooks (Telegram, Slack, etc.) can reach it. `none` = keep it local, no tunnel; `cloudflare` = Cloudflare Tunnel via cloudflared (needs a Zero Trust account and token); `tailscale` = Tailscale Funnel/Serve (tailnet-only or public, no account beyond tailscale); `ngrok` = ngrok agent with auth token; `openvpn` = bring-your-own OpenVPN egress; `pinggy` = Pinggy SSH tunnels (quick one-shot URLs); `custom` = run an arbitrary command you define under `[tunnel.custom]`.

allowed_users.description: Allowed usernames or user IDs. Empty = deny all, "*" = allow all

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

VerifiableIntentConfig.description: Verifiable Intent (VI) credential verification and issuance (`[verifiable_intent]` section).

enabled.description: Enable VI credential verification on commerce tool calls (default: false).

strictness.description:
Strictness mode for constraint evaluation: "strict" (fail-closed on unknown
constraint types) or "permissive" (skip unknown types with a warning).
Default: "strict".

VoiceDuplexConfig.description:
Voice duplex configuration (`[channels.voice_duplex]`).

Enables full-duplex voice event handling over WebSocket.
When disabled (default), voice events are rejected as unknown types.

enabled.description:
Enable full-duplex voice event handling over WebSocket.
Default: false. When false, voice events are rejected as unknown types.

WatiConfig.description: WATI WhatsApp Business API channel configuration.

allowed_numbers.description: Allowed phone numbers (E.164 format) or "*" for all.

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

proxy_url.description:
Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).
Overrides the global `[proxy]` setting for this channel only.

allowed_users.description: Allowed user IDs. Empty = deny all, "*" = allow all

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

webhook_key.description: Webhook key from WeCom Bot configuration

WebAuthnConfig.description:
WebAuthn / FIDO2 hardware key authentication configuration (`[security.webauthn]`).

Enables registration and authentication via hardware security keys
(YubiKey, SoloKey, etc.) and platform authenticators (Touch ID, Windows Hello).

enabled.description: Enable WebAuthn authentication. Default: false.

rp_id.description: Relying Party ID (domain name, e.g. "example.com"). Default: "localhost".

WebFetchConfig.description:
Web fetch tool configuration (`[web_fetch]` section).

Fetches web pages and converts HTML to plain text for LLM consumption.
Domain filtering: `allowed_domains` controls which hosts are reachable (use `["*"]`
for all public hosts). `blocked_domains` takes priority over `allowed_domains`.
If `allowed_domains` is empty, all requests are rejected (deny-by-default).

allowed_domains.description: Allowed domains for web fetch (exact or subdomain match; `["*"]` = all public hosts)

allowed_private_hosts.description: Private/internal hosts allowed to bypass SSRF protection (e.g. `["192.168.1.10", "internal.local"]`)

blocked_domains.description: Blocked domains (exact or subdomain match; always takes priority over allowed_domains)

enabled.description: Enable `web_fetch` tool for fetching web page content

max_response_size.description: Maximum response size in bytes (default: 500KB, plain text is much smaller than raw HTML)

brave_api_key.description: Brave Search API key (required if provider is "brave")

enabled.description: Enable `web_search_tool` for web searches

searxng_instance_url.description: SearXNG instance URL (required if provider is `"searxng"`), e.g. `"https://searx.example.com"`.

WebhookAuditConfig.description:
Configuration for the webhook-audit builtin hook.

Sends an HTTP POST with a JSON body to an external endpoint each time
a tool call matches one of the configured patterns. Useful for
centralised audit logging, SIEM ingestion, or compliance pipelines.

enabled.description: Enable the webhook-audit hook. Default: `false`.

include_args.description:
Include tool call arguments in the audit payload. Default: `false`.

Be mindful of sensitive data — arguments may contain secrets or PII.

max_args_bytes.description:
Maximum size (in bytes) of serialised arguments included in a single
audit payload. Arguments exceeding this limit are truncated.
Default: `4096`.

tool_patterns.description:
Glob patterns for tool names to audit (e.g. `["Bash", "Write"]`).
An empty list means **no** tools are audited.

url.description: Target URL that will receive the audit POST requests.

WebhookConfig.description:
Webhook channel configuration.

Receives messages via HTTP POST and sends replies to a configurable outbound URL.
This is the "universal adapter" for any system that supports webhooks.

auth_header.description: Optional `Authorization` header value for outbound requests.

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

port.description: Port to listen on for incoming webhooks.

secret.description: Optional shared secret for webhook signature verification (HMAC-SHA256).

send_method.description: HTTP method for outbound messages (`POST` or `PUT`). Default: `POST`.

send_url.description: URL to POST/PUT outbound messages to.

WhatsAppChatPolicy.description:
Policy for a particular WhatsApp chat type (DMs or groups) when
`mode = "personal"`.

oneOf.description: Only respond to senders on the `allowed_numbers` list (default).
Ignore all messages in this chat type.
Respond to every message regardless of allowlist.

WhatsAppConfig.description:
WhatsApp channel configuration (Cloud API or Web mode).

Set `phone_number_id` for Cloud API mode, or `session_path` for Web mode.

access_token.description: Access token from Meta Business Suite (Cloud API mode)

allowed_numbers.description: Allowed phone numbers (E.164 format: +1234567890) or "*" for all

app_secret.description:
App secret from Meta Business Suite (for webhook signature verification)
Can also be set via `ZEROCLAW_WHATSAPP_APP_SECRET` environment variable
Only used in Cloud API mode

approval_timeout_secs.description: Seconds to wait for operator approval on `always_ask` tools before auto-denying.

dm_mention_patterns.description:
Regex patterns for DM mention gating (case-insensitive).
When non-empty, only direct messages matching at least one pattern are
processed; matched fragments are stripped from the forwarded content.
Example: `["@?ZeroClaw", "\\+?15555550123"]`

dm_policy.description:
Policy for direct messages when mode = "personal".
"allowlist" (default) | "ignore" | "all".

enabled.description: Whether this channel is active (must be explicitly enabled). Default: false.

group_mention_patterns.description:
Regex patterns for group-chat mention gating (case-insensitive).
When non-empty, only group messages matching at least one pattern are
processed; matched fragments are stripped from the forwarded content.
Example: `["@?ZeroClaw", "\\+?15555550123"]`

group_policy.description:
Policy for group chats when mode = "personal".
"allowlist" (default) | "ignore" | "all".

mention_only.description:
When true, only respond to messages that @-mention the bot in groups (Web mode only).
Direct messages are always processed.
Bot identity is resolved from the wa-rs device at runtime; `pair_phone` seeds it on first connect.

mode.description:
Usage mode for WhatsApp Web: "business" (default) or "personal".
In personal mode the bot applies dm_policy, group_policy, and
self_chat_mode to decide which chats to respond in.

pair_code.description:
Custom pair code for linking (Web mode, optional)
Leave empty to let WhatsApp generate one

pair_phone.description:
Phone number for pair code linking (Web mode, optional)
Format: country code + number (e.g., "15551234567")
If not set, QR code pairing will be used

phone_number_id.description: Phone number ID from Meta Business API (Cloud API mode)

proxy_url.description:
Per-channel proxy URL (http, https, socks5, socks5h).
Overrides the global `[proxy]` setting for this channel only.

self_chat_mode.description:
When true and mode = "personal", always respond to messages in the
user's own self-chat (Notes to Self). Defaults to false.

session_path.description:
Session database path for WhatsApp Web client (Web mode)
When set, enables native WhatsApp Web mode with wa-rs

verify_token.description:
Webhook verify token (you define this, Meta sends it back for verification)
Only used in Cloud API mode

WhatsAppWebMode.description:
WhatsApp Web usage mode.

`Personal` treats the account as a personal phone — the bot only responds to
incoming messages that pass the DM/group/self-chat policy filters.
`Business` (default) responds to all incoming messages, subject only to the
`allowed_numbers` allowlist.

oneOf.description: Respond to all messages passing the allowlist (default).
Apply per-chat-type policies (dm_policy, group_policy, self_chat_mode).

active_workspace.description: Which workspace profile is currently active — picks the `<workspaces_dir>/<name>/` directory ZeroClaw reads from and writes to. Required when multi-workspace is enabled; ignored otherwise.

cross_workspace_search.description: Let memory search span all workspaces instead of only the active one. Off by default — turning it on defeats the point of isolation and is only useful for global admin queries.

enabled.description: Turn on multi-workspace profiles — each named engagement gets its own memory, secrets, and audit directories so work for one client/project never bleeds into another. Leave off for single-workspace mode where everything lives under `~/.zeroclaw/workspace`.

isolate_audit.description: Give each profile its own tool-call and channel-message audit trail, so you can hand off logs for a single engagement without exposing other work.

isolate_memory.description: Give each profile its own `brain.db` so conversation history, notes, and memories from one engagement don't leak into another. Turn off only if you want all profiles sharing a single memory store.

isolate_secrets.description: Scope provider API keys, channel tokens, and other secrets to the active profile — so a key added while on `client-a` isn't visible from `client-b`. Turn off only if you want all profiles sharing one secret namespace.

workspaces_dir.description: Parent directory holding all workspace profiles, one subdirectory per profile. Override to keep profiles on a separate disk or inside an encrypted volume.

bearerAuth.description: Pairing-derived bearer token. Printed at gateway startup.

info.description: Per-property CRUD endpoints over the same `Config` mutation core that `zeroclaw config get/set/list/init/migrate` uses on the CLI. See https://github.com/zeroclaw-labs/zeroclaw/issues/6175 for the full surface and acceptance checklist.
info.title: ZeroClaw Gateway — Config CRUD

patch.description:
Operations execute in order against an in-memory copy; `Config::validate()` runs once at the end; on success the snapshot persists and swaps. On failure, on-disk and in-memory state are unchanged. `move`/`copy` return `op_not_supported`. `test` against a secret path returns `secret_test_forbidden`.

**Drift guard:** if the on-disk file has drifted from in-memory state on any path being patched, returns 409 `config_changed_externally` unless the request carries `X-ZeroClaw-Override-Drift: true`. GET /api/config/drift to inspect first.

parameters.description: Set to `true` to overwrite externally-edited values without confirmation.
parameters.name: X-ZeroClaw-Override-Drift

200.description: All operations applied and config saved.

400.description: Validation, type, or operation error. See ConfigApiError.code.

404.description: Path not found in the schema.

409.description: On-disk config drifted from in-memory state.

patch.summary: Apply a JSON Patch (RFC 6902) document atomically

get.description: Returns properties whose in-memory values differ from what's on disk now. Empty when they agree. Secret entries carry only `{path, secret: true, drifted: true}`; values never leave the server.
get.summary: Drift between in-memory and on-disk config

parameters.description: Section prefix to scope the init pass (e.g. `providers`).
parameters.name: section

200.description: Initialized section names (empty when nothing was uninitialized).

get.description: Returns every reachable path with its type, category, and onboard section. Secret entries carry `{populated, is_secret: true}` and no value.

parameters.description: Optional prefix to scope the listing.
parameters.name: prefix

post.description: Mirrors `zeroclaw config migrate`. Backs up the previous file as `config.toml.bak` before writing.

200.description: Migration applied (or already at the current schema version).

post.summary: Apply on-disk schema migration in place

404.description: Path not found in the schema.

delete.summary: Reset one property to its default

get.description: Returns the user value for non-secret fields. For secret fields, returns `{path, populated}` only — never the value, length, or any encoded form.

404.description: Path not found in the schema.

put.description: Validates the resulting whole-config state, persists, and swaps in-memory. For secret fields, response carries `{populated: true}` only.

404.description: Path not found in the schema.
